chapters.chapter_summary: The patient was positioned supine with the right shoulder elevated and the head turned to the left. General anesthesia was administered.
A circular bone flap was elevated using a single burr hole, exposing the underlying relaxed dura. The dura was then opened to expose the tumor.

The tumor was carefully dissected from surrounding brain tissue using microscissors and forceps. Sodium fluorescein dye was used to identify residual cancerous cells, and additional tumors were discovered and excised.
The tumor was removed in one piece. The surgical field was inspected for any residual tumor, and additional dissection was performed as necessary.
Hydrogen peroxide-soaked cotton balls were applied to the resection site for hemostasis and destruction of neoplastic cells. The surgical cavity was covered with a hemostatic agent.
The dura was re-approximated and duroplasty was completed. The bone flap was re-approximated and synthetic filler material was placed between the bone flap and surrounding cranial structures.
The patient had no neurological deficit post-surgery and was discharged home. Post-operative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.

operative_note.title: Temporal Parietal Craniotomy for Tumor Resection

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a temporal parietal craniotomy for the resection of a large tumor at the temporo-parietal junction. The patient presented with a history of headache, progressive weakness on the left side of the body, and difficulty with gait and balance.
SOAP.Objective: I identified a large mass at the temporo-parietal junction with significant local mass effect and vasogenic edema. I observed the tumor being easily distinguished from the normal brain upon opening of the dura. I used sodium fluorescein dye to confirm the location of the tumor and to identify residual cancerous cells.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful with the tumor being removed in one piece. I identified and excised additional tumors near the initial resection site. Post-operative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers. The patient had no neurological deficit post-surgery and was discharged home. Follow-up MRI scans were planned to monitor for any recurrence.
